text: Moving from the gathering space into this exhibit, I'm struck by the way our environments shape not only our interactions but our perceptions of one another. The conversation I overheard lingers in my mind—the notion that our environments profoundly influence how we react and relate is so present here. This second space, with its curated aesthetic and emphasis on research and development, feels both inviting and artificial. I am aware of my own presence as an observer, almost placed between worlds: the quiet anticipation of the group, and the reflective, surreal atmosphere of the installation. I feel contemplative, as though caught in a moment that is both ordinary and deeply meaningful, prompting me to wonder how much the spaces we move through direct our thoughts and feelings toward each other.
